DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-22-2002 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Mark G. Bonino, Esq. of ROPERS, MAJESKI 408/947-4886, for defendants Truck Insurance Exchange and Arthur Robinson, demurring party
Bari S. Robinson, Esq., 510/465-4747, for plaintiff and opposing party
* ARGUED and the Court adopted its tentative ruling:
DEFENDANTs TRUCK INSURANCE EXCHANGE and Arthur Robinson'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT: Demurrer as to 1st c/a (fraud) - SUSTAINED with leave to amend, no sufficient allegation of representation by Truck Insurance Exchange or Robinson to plaintiff; as to 5th c/a (conversion) - SUSTAINED with leave to amend, no sufficient allegation and triable issue of fact exists or Robinson obtained the funds to further own benefit; as to 6th c/a (negligent hiring) - SUSTAINED with leave, no obligation as to alleged facts in negligently in hiring Robinson; as to 7th c/a - SUSTAINED with leave, no adequate allegation of outrageous conduct; 8th c/a (negligent infliction) - SUSTAINED with leave to repleade with independent cause of action such as covenent of good faith and fair dealing; 11th c/a conspiracy - OVERRULED. Except 11th c/a, all others sustained with 15 days leave to amend. (AJR/301/KS) Reporter: Patricia M. Evezich, CSR #1193

MOTION Notice Of Hearing On Demurres And Demurrers To First Amended Complaint;
Off Calendar Jun-18-2002 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Bari S. Robinson, Esq. 510/625-1507 for Plaintiffs and opposing party

Counsel requested a cause of action for breach of contract be added as to WASHING MUTUAL. So ordered by the Court. Tentative ruling adopted as well.
Defendant Washington Mutual Bank's Demurrer to First Amended Complaint SUSTAINED without leave to amend:
--- as to the First Cause of Action for Fraud and
--- the Seventh Cause of Action for Intentional infliction of Emotional Distress.
OVERRULED as to:
--- the Eighth Cause of Action for Negligent Infliction of Emotional Distress and
--- the Thirteenth Cause of Action for Negligence.
(AJR/KH) Lee Batara, CSR 3759

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-18-2002 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Bari S. Robinson, Esq. 510/625-1507 for Plaintiffs and opposing party

Counsel requested a cause of action for breach of contract be added as to defendant WASHINGTON MUTUAL. So ordered by the Court. In addition, tentative ruling adopted -- Defendant Arthur Robinson's Demurrer:
SUSTAINED without leave to amend as to
--- the Second Cause of Action for Negligent Misrepresentation,
--- the Tenth Cause of Action for Breach of Fiduciary Duty,
--- the Eleventh Cause of Action for Breach of Covenant of Good Faith and Fair Dealing and Unfair Claims Practices.

SUSTAINED with leave to amend as to:
--- the First Cause of Action for Fraud,
--- the Fifth Cause of Action for Conversion,
--- the Twelfth Cause of Action for Conspiracy,
--- the Seventh Cause of Action for Intentional Infliction of Emotional Distress and
--- the Eighth Cause of action for Negligent Infliction of Emotional Distress
(AJR/KH) Lee Batara, CSR 3759
